title: Level 120
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 120: "Chokepoint"}}{{Blurbackground|file=Image 2026-03-28 235525240.png|opacity=0.5}} {{Class Habitable}} Level 120 is the 121st level of the Backrooms.


[[File:Image 2026-03-28 235525240.png|center|link=|thumb|500x500px|


'''''A view of Level 120 through an entrance created by a Gate Carver.]]'''''
{{Blurimage|file=Image 2026-03-28 235114394.png|caption=An image of Level 120.}} == Description == Level 120 is a 153 km x 137 km, closed megastructure with buildings of varying scale and density. Predominantly Brutalist, the level encompasses many circulation spaces, such as hallways, lobbies, plazas, and stairs.Estimated through satellite-style drone mapping. Their siting often contradicts their intended purpose (e.g., staircases that abruptly end, plunging into deep shafts). By contrast, an 80 km^2 area in the northwestern part of the level is modified with the vernacular preferences of its occupants. All architectural anomalies have been patched, removed, or restricted. This is due to the significant presence of New Banbury in that section, which is one of the largest settlements in the Backrooms, governed by the Democratic Survivalists Party (DSP).The DSP maintains tight centralized control over New Banbury’s administration despite its name. It still holds elections every 8 years, however. Accordingly, this is the level’s safest area if one is allowed entry into the base. {{Blurimage|file=Dmitry-kropachev-0f7saqvyojY-unsplash.jpg|caption=An image of The Plaza.}} Although the level fluctuates in appearance, it has a consistent, open, primary area known as “The Plaza.” It is a continuous interior expanse that is never fully divided by a wall, where the facades of its contained structures are made visible. Functioning as the main load-bearing framework, this enclosure is defined by a perimeter-supported exposed truss system that spans the entire structure like a continuous mantle. The exterior envelope is clad in a seamless, high-gloss glass surface. Additionally, reports from outdoor observations and construction projects within the base determined that the enclosure comprised sporadically interconnected amorphous volumes, though other potentially existing structures are extremely difficult to perceive. So, whether Level 120’s edifice floats or is held up by support beams remains at large. Some apertures also appear only within specific, inaccessible areas caused by architectural anomalies. Beyond this truss boundary, a seemingly endless sky is present. This is where the level’s edifice floats within, and the existence of other structures within this area remains unknown. Analogous to [[the Frontrooms|Earth]], the sky has a consistent 24-hour diurnal cycle, though missing both the sun and the moon. Instead, the sources of daylight and nocturnal illumination originate from unidentified sources. Meteorological phenomena mirror those of Earth, which still appear; for example, clouds, rain, rainbows, thunderstorms, and stars have all been observed. Furthermore, no natural disasters have been recorded. The structure sustains virtually no weather-related damage as the glass is never seen to have any imperfections on its surface. {{Blurimage|file=Max-titov--U4Aajm2hT4-unsplash.jpg|caption=A set of twin skyscrapers. They are not inhabited.}} Conditions within the edifice remain climatically stable year-round, with no conventional seasons. Instead, it follows an equatorial regime (i.e., only consisting of wet and dry seasons lasting roughly six months each). However, these periods alternate monthly rather than occurring consecutively, beginning with the wet season in January, then the dry season in February, and so on. Temperature and humidity remain nearly constant throughout the year, however, ranging between 23.5–32.5 °C and 80–85% relative humidity. Average rainfall measures 70–100 mm during dry periods and approximately 140–160 mm during wet periods.These measurements were collected via numerous sources, though mainly by hidden atmospheric monitoring stations that the administration of New Banbury had installed. ==Entities== Permanent habitation of hostile entities in Level 120 is extinct. Although the presence of abandoned “gates” opened by Gate Carvers allows a few of them to enter from other levels, the absence of prey and food sources, as well as pervasive surveillance and security, has rendered long-term habitation within the level impossible. The cause of extinction remains unknown. However, still-decomposing carcasses and skeletons of entities have been observed scattered outside the New Banbury area, without any indication of injuries caused by external forces. This suggests a gradual extinction event, as the level’s area is extremely small compared to other regions of the Backrooms, leading to prey populations declining exponentially and the collapse of the level’s food chain.Entities mostly evolved within levels that are millions of kilometers wide, meaning the extremely small size of Level 120 compared to other levels prevented sustainable predator populations. Docile entities, on the other hand, often wander Level 120 aimlessly, though their numbers are still limited (except Facelings and Kats) due to New Banbury forces frequently relocating them to their origin through [[Object 9|Gate Carvers]].Gate Carvers are devices that can open direct passages between levels. They mostly come from other levels, such as Counter Entities, Facelings, Hydrolitis Plague, Kats, and Refuses, and certain entities (i.e., Kats) may even live permanently as pets. Surprisingly, Level 120 also affects these non-hostile entities that normally become aggressive when provoked, erasing that effect. They act passively not only toward humans, but from other entities as well. This may disable their response to obvious dangers, such as an attack from a hostile entity, despite bearing traits contradicting that idleness. Nevertheless, this phenomenon does not apply to naturally hostile entities. ==Colonies & Outposts== New Banbury is the largest outpost in Level 120. It is located 62 kilometers away to the northwest of the general area where wanderers typically arrive on the level. It covers approximately 80 km^2 and is one of the most populous autocratic settlements in the Backrooms, with 8,500 people. Although it is generally open to newcomers, one may be sent to a marginalized district that lies within the lower levels of the level if one does not pass the requirements to be a resident, such as being majorly physically or mentally disfigured. There exists signage that assists wanderers in finding the base, though one can also use a compass, as the device functions in the level.Unlike other levels, magnetic readings in Level 120 are stable and consistent across the entire structure. ==Entrances & Exits== ===Entrances=== Most entrances to Level 120 are one-way passages. * Although access is uncommon, pre-programmed [[Object 9|Gate Carvers]] can be used in any level of the Backrooms to enter Level 120. * No-clipping through the end of a hallway in [[Level 1]] may lead one here. * Very rarely, [[Level 2]]'s hallways may "drop" infinitely into a dark horizon. Jumping downward has a chance of transporting one either to [[The Void]] or to Level 120, though one may sustain leg injuries upon entering. * Storage cabinets in the apartments of [[Level 72]] sometimes have trapdoors on the floor, which bring one to this level. * Wooden crates in [[Level 96]] can act as direct passageways to Level 120. ===Exits=== Gateways spawned using Gate Carvers are the only way to exit Level 120, as New Banbury personnel actively restrict or destroy known ones. == References == {{Footnotes}} {{CSS|User:SherlsF/canon.css}} __notoc__ __noeditsection__ [[Category:Winter's Butterfly]] [[Category:Canons]] [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Safe]] [[Category:Cluster I]]
